message:
fix(fr-store): function_load rejects unregistered engine names with upstream wording (br-frankenredis-r85v)

Self-review caught a third real defect in the FUNCTION load
path: function_load accepted any engine name in the meta header
(uppercased) and silently treated everything as LUA. Upstream
functions.c::functionsCreateWithLibraryCtx (emit site at
functions.c:983) returns 'Engine '<name>' not found' when the
header references an unregistered engine. Loading a script with
'#!python name=lib' should fail loudly, not store a fake
LUA-flagged library.

Add the engine gate after the header parse: only 'LUA' is
accepted (matching script_lua.c::LUA_ENGINE_NAME). Any other
engine name returns the upstream wording.

New test function_load_rejects_unregistered_engine_with_upstream_wording
pins the rejection wording for python and confirms LUA still
loads.

fr-store 292 passed; fr-runtime 281 passed; fr-command 656
passed; fr-conformance 184 passed; cargo clippy --tests
-D warnings clean; cargo fmt --check clean.

Slice of br-frankenredis-r85v.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
